behavior_summary: This script implements egg-related trigger logic used to manage door/slider activation when an egg 'hatches' and simple housekeeping when an actor leaves the egg's fast-area trigger.

U8P_DOOREGG::leaveFastArea acts as cleanup: it reads the egg's eggId, detects a previously-applied +0x64 (100) marker that denotes an active/hatched state, subtracts 0x64 to restore the base id, and writes the result back (undoes the active marker).

U8P_DOOREGG::hatch (helper) is responsible for the complex hatch behavior: it branches on eggId (special-case 0xC8/200 and comparisons around 0x64/100), may set the egg as active by adding 0x64, scans nearby tiles/items for matches by attributes (id/frame/shape/quality), spawns door or slider scripts for matched items, and sets a 0x0400 item-quality bit on some matched items to mark them handled.
notable_control_flow: U8P_DOOREGG::leaveFastArea: single conditional branch — if eggId has the 0x64 marker, compute eggId - 0x64 and call Egg::setEggId; otherwise do nothing. This is straightforward cleanup.

hatch: multi-branch control flow — special case when eggId == 0xC8 (200) that triggers a distinct scanning/spawn path; otherwise if eggId < 0x64 it marks the egg active by adding 0x64; subsequently performs several iterative scans over nearby items with conditional spawns (DOOR_NS or SLIDER variants) and item-quality updates.
Spawning behavior is used to delegate door/slider animation or activation to other scripts (spawn calls return a temp that is popped/discarded).
open_questions: Precise semantic meaning of eggId numeric ranges and the magic constants 0x64 (100) and 0xC8 (200) — they appear to be state markers (base id vs. active/hatched marker) but exact mapping to gameplay content is inferred.
Exact gameplay effect of setting the item quality bit 0x0400 — likely a 'processed/linked' flag so the same item isn't processed twice, but that is not explicitly documented.
Whether spawn DOOR_NS::08B4 always opens a door or sometimes only animates it; likewise the exact difference between SLIDER::0080 and SLIDER::01A1 variants is inferred from context but not certain.

Whether the pid passed into some spawn calls is always the actor who triggered the hatch or sometimes another process id; the role of the pid in the door spawn is likely contextual for permissions/ownership.

main_function.name: U8P_DOOREGG::leaveFastArea
main_function.purpose: Housekeeping when an actor leaves the egg's fast-area trigger: if the egg's eggId contains the +0x64 active/hatched marker, remove that marker by subtracting 0x64 and writing the resulting eggId back.
entry_conditions: Called on fast-area exit (U8P_DOOREGG::leaveFastArea event) for the egg object instance.
Operates on the egg instance referenced by the method call (egg_object passed implicitly at BP+06h).

function_map.name: U8P_DOOREGG::leaveFastArea
function_map.role: Fast-area exit handler that clears an active/hatched marker from the egg's eggId.

function_map.name: U8P_DOOREGG::hatch
function_map.role: Handles the egg hatch event: marks the egg active, scans nearby items, spawns door/slider scripts, and marks processed items.

function_map.name: Egg::getEggId
function_map.role: Read the egg object's eggId (state field) used for branching and comparisons.

function_map.name: Egg::setEggId
function_map.role: Write a new eggId for the egg object (used to add/remove the 0x64 active marker).

function_map.name: Item::getQuality
function_map.role: Read an item's quality/flags word used to test attributes and mask bits during scans.

function_map.name: Item::setQuality
function_map.role: Write back a modified quality/flags word (used to OR in marker 0x0400 on matched items).

function_map.name: spawn DOOR_NS::08B4
function_map.role: Spawn/activate a north/south door-related script for a matched item (likely to animate/open the door).

function_map.name: spawn SLIDER::0080
function_map.role: Spawn a slider-related script variant for matched items.

function_map.name: spawn SLIDER::01A1
function_map.role: Spawn an alternate slider-related script variant for a different class of matched items.

external_calls.purpose: Read current eggId to detect whether the +0x64 active marker is present.
Write eggId - 0x64 back to the egg to remove the active/hatched marker when the actor leaves the trigger.
Read eggId to determine hatch branch (special-case 0xC8, whether to add 0x64 marker, and to compare against nearby item attributes).
Mark the egg active by writing eggId + 0x64 (when appropriate branch is taken).
Read candidate items' quality flags to filter matches and decide whether to spawn scripts or set the 0x0400 flag.
Write back item quality after OR-ing in 0x0400 to mark items as processed/linked.
Spawn a door-related process for matched door items (likely opens/activates the door); sometimes called with item pointer and triggering pid.
Spawn a slider process for matched slider-type items.
Spawn an alternate slider process for a different matched item category.

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: This script implements egg-related trigger logic used to manage door/slider activation when an egg 'hatches' and simple housekeeping when an actor leaves the egg's fast-area trigger.
